pair_prompt_prefix: These two movie reviews express the same sentiment - either both are positive or both are negative. Review 1:

positive: this is a clearly positive movie review
the reviewer praises the film and recommends it
an enthusiastic, favorable review of the movie
negative: this is a clearly negative movie review
the reviewer criticizes the film and dislikes it
an unfavorable, dismissive review of the movie

cascade_form: Option E (staging table movie.q567_candidates with K_pos+K_neg most-confident reviews); original sembench Q5.sql runs verbatim with table-name swap

baseline._status_note: Baseline NOT re-run on our project. A prior attempt observed wall=301.6s (BQ slot starvation; paper reports 54.5s) and BQ returned 10 pairs but the script crashed before F1 evaluation due to an int() bug on the string id column. Per project policy (skip baseline if too slow → use paper numbers), we substitute paper Table 4(a) values as the canonical baseline reference
baseline.method: sembench bigquery/Q5.sql verbatim on movie.reviews — NOT EXECUTED in this run
baseline.sql:
SELECT r1.id, r1.reviewId AS reviewId1, r2.reviewId AS reviewId2
    FROM movie.reviews AS r1
    JOIN movie.reviews AS r2
    ON r1.id = r2.id AND r1.reviewId <> r2.reviewId
    WHERE r1.id = 'ant_man_and_the_wasp_quantumania' AND AI.IF(
      ('These two movie reviews express the same sentiment - either both are positive or both are negative. Review 1: ', r1.reviewText, ', Review 2: ', r2.reviewText),
      connection_id => 'us.connection',
      endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash'
    )
    LIMIT 10

cost_breakdown._source: paper Table 4(a) cost; n_calls back-derived using our per_pair calibration

cascade.method: Option E: dase top K_pos +highest margin, top K_neg -lowest margin → CTAS staging → original Q5 SQL on staging

stage1_ctas.sql:
CREATE OR REPLACE TABLE movie.q567_candidates AS
    SELECT * FROM movie.reviews
    WHERE reviewId IN (102781777,102780006,102779524,102780625,102779496,102781269,102780627,102780917,102779743,102779555)

stage2_run.sql:
SELECT r1.id, r1.reviewId AS reviewId1, r2.reviewId AS reviewId2
    FROM movie.q567_candidates AS r1
    JOIN movie.q567_candidates AS r2
    ON r1.id = r2.id AND r1.reviewId <> r2.reviewId
    WHERE r1.id = 'ant_man_and_the_wasp_quantumania' AND AI.IF(
      ('These two movie reviews express the same sentiment - either both are positive or both are negative. Review 1: ', r1.reviewText, ', Review 2: ', r2.reviewText),
      connection_id => 'us.connection',
      endpoint => 'gemini-2.5-flash'
    )
    LIMIT 10

cost_breakdown.n_llm_calls_method: min(max(round(slot_ms/2500), |returned|), staging_pairs); BQ short-circuits at LIMIT 10
